 {"id":"gt-r8ej","title":"Implement pinned beads for handoff state","description":"Add pinned bead support to beads:
- Pinned beads never close, only update
- Use for persistent state like Refinery handoffs
- bd create --pinned flag
- bd list --pinned to find them
- Update description to change state","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T18:09:08.019293-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T18:09:08.019293-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-r8ej","depends_on_id":"gt-ktal","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T18:09:39.340915-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-rana","title":"Patrol System: Agent lifecycle loops with attachments","description":"Enable Gas Town agents to run continuous patrols, survive crashes, and hand off work across sessions.

## Core Concepts
- **Attachments**: Molecules bound to agent's pinned bead until complete
- **Patrols**: Cyclic molecules that loop (deacon, witness, refinery)
- **Quiescent**: Agents that sleep until triggered (witness, refinery)

## Design Doc
See docs/patrol-system-design.md

## Phases
Phase 1: Foundation (attachment field, daemon detection, mol-deacon-patrol)
Phase 2: Quiescent Agents (wake triggers, witness/refinery patrols)
Phase 3: Callbacks and Plugins (mail protocol, plugin runner)
Phase 4: Polish (gt patrol status, metrics, tuning)

## Key Decisions
- Attachment as field on pinned bead (not edge type, for now)
- Mail-based orchestration for all callbacks
- Queue replacement for heartbeat delivery (not pile-up)
- Burn and respawn for patrol loops (not in-place reset)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:23.416949-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:23.416949-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-rana.1","title":"Phase 1.1: Attachment field on pinned beads","description":"Add attached_molecule field to pinned bead schema.

## Schema Change
Add to handoff/pinned bead issues:
- attached_molecule: string (root issue ID of attached mol)
- attached_at: timestamp

## Implementation
- Update beads Issue struct if needed
- Or: use labels/metadata field
- Ensure bd show displays attachment info

## Acceptance
- Can set/clear attachment on a bead
- bd show displays attachment status","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:34.251531-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:34.251531-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-rana.1","depends_on_id":"gt-rana","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:34.253378-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-rana.2","title":"Phase 1.2: Daemon attachment detection","description":"Daemon polls Deacon's pinned bead to detect naked state.

## Implementation
- Daemon knows Deacon's pinned bead ID
- Every heartbeat cycle: bd show <pinned> --json
- Parse attached_molecule field
- If null/empty: Deacon is naked

## Actions on Naked
- Spawn mol-deacon-patrol
- Attach to Deacon's pinned
- Nudge Deacon to start

## Failsafes
- Keepalive file monitoring
- Stale detection (>10min no progress)
- Force nudge on stale

Depends: gt-rana.1","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:53.520213-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:53.520213-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-rana.2","depends_on_id":"gt-rana","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:53.521672-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-rana.2","depends_on_id":"gt-rana.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:39:11.333008-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-rana.3","title":"Phase 1.3: mol-deacon-patrol definition","description":"Define the Deacon patrol molecule in builtin_molecules.go.

## Steps
1. inbox-check - Handle callbacks from agents
2. health-scan - Ping Witnesses and Refineries
3. plugin-run - Execute registered plugins
4. orphan-check - Find abandoned work
5. session-gc - Clean dead sessions
6. context-check - Check own context limit
7. loop-or-exit - Burn and let daemon respawn, or exit if context high

## Implementation
- Add DeaconPatrolMolecule() to builtin_molecules.go
- Add to BuiltinMolecules() list
- Test with bd mol show mol-deacon-patrol","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:54.555938-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:54.555938-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-rana.3","depends_on_id":"gt-rana","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:54.55744-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-rana.4","title":"Phase 1.4: Basic patrol runner in Deacon","description":"Deacon CLAUDE.md and prime context for patrol execution.

## Deacon Context
- Update Deacon CLAUDE.md with patrol instructions
- On wake: check pinned bead for attachment
- If attached: resume molecule from current step
- Execute steps, close each when done
- On final step: burn molecule, go naked

## gt prime Enhancement
- Detect if agent has attached molecule
- Show molecule progress in prime output
- Include patrol-specific instructions

+{"id":"gt-rana.5","title":"Phase 2: Quiescent agents (Witness, Refinery)","description":"Enable Witness and Refinery to sleep until triggered.

## Scope
- Wake triggers: gt spawn, MR submit, mail, Deacon ping
- Wake SLA: <1 minute
- mol-witness-patrol and mol-refinery-patrol definitions
- Quiescent entry/exit protocol

## Implementation
- Kill tmux session on quiescent entry
- Preserve sandbox (worktree, state files)
- Restart session on wake trigger
- Agent checks pinned, spawns default patrol if naked

+{"id":"gt-rana.6","title":"Phase 3: Callbacks and plugins","description":"Mail-based callback protocol and plugin surface.

## Callbacks
- Polecat → Witness: shutdown requests
- Witness → Deacon: escalations, status
- Crew → Deacon: recycle requests
- Deacon → Mayor: escalations

## Plugins
- .beads/plugins.yaml registry
- Cooldown tracking
- Plugin runner in deacon patrol

## Commands
- gt patrol status - Show patrol state for all agents
- gt patrol history - Recent patrol activity

## Observability
- Metrics collection
- Alert thresholds
- Dashboard (optional)

## Tuning
- Cooldown optimization
- Wake SLA verification
- Error threshold tuning

 {"id":"gt-rixa","title":"Bug: parseLifecycleRequest always matches 'cycle' due to LIFECYCLE prefix","description":"In lifecycle.go, parseLifecycleRequest checks strings.Contains(title, "cycle") first, but the prefix "LIFECYCLE:" contains the word "cycle". This means ALL lifecycle messages match the cycle action, making restart and shutdown unreachable. Fix: check for restart/shutdown before cycle, or use word boundaries.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-19T16:17:16.083512-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T16:17:24.480543-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-rm3","title":"CLI: gt refinery commands (start, stop, status, queue)","description":"CLI commands for managing the Refinery agent.

## Commands

```bash
gt refinery start <rig>      # Start refinery for a rig
gt refinery stop <rig>       # Stop refinery
gt refinery status <rig>     # Show refinery status
gt refinery queue <rig>      # Show merge queue
```

## gt refinery start

Starts the Refinery daemon for the specified rig.

Options:
- --foreground: Run in foreground (default: background)
- --auto-merge: Enable auto-merge (default: from config)

## gt refinery stop

Stops a running Refinery. Gracefully finishes current MR if processing.

## gt refinery status

Shows:
- Running state (running/stopped)
- Current MR being processed (if any)
- Queue length
- Last merge time
- Recent activity

## gt refinery queue

Shows the merge queue:
```
Merge queue for 'wyvern':
  1. [pending] Toast/polecat-auth-fix (15m ago)
  2. [pending] Capable/polecat-new-feature (5m ago)
  
1 merged today, 0 rejected
```

## Implementation

Uses gt-ov2 (Refinery agent) for daemon functionality.","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:24.754361-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T14:16:34.593637-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T14:16:34.593637-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-rm3","depends_on_id":"gt-ov2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:30.679909-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-rr1i","title":"mol-swarm-cleanup: Post-swarm debris cleanup molecule","description":"After a 20+ worker swarm completed, found significant beads debris:
- 18 stale messages (work assignments, lifecycle requests, swarm instructions)
- 3 completed issues still open/in_progress
- Test messages accumulated

Need: Document a post-swarm checklist or create gt swarm cleanup command that:
1. Closes stale work assignment messages
2. Reviews in_progress issues for completion
3. Closes orphaned lifecycle messages
4. Optionally archives test messages","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:12:28.646175-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:15:45.521085-08:00"}
